task: I need to send a short note to a coworker about a schedule change, and I want to make sure it includes the key details without getting messy. Can you help me put it together so it’s easy to send?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, clear version you can send:

**Hi [Name], just a quick heads-up that the schedule has changed. [Insert key detail]. Let me know if you have any questions. Thanks.**

If you want, I can also help you make it:
- a little more casual
- more professional
- or fill it in with your exact schedule details

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, close — but I need it a little more specific, not just the template. I want something short I can actually send without sounding vague.

Can you give me 2-3 versions for a coworker? Like:
- professional
- casual but still respectful
- very brief/text style

And keep it clean. I don’t want fluff.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are 3 clean options you can send:

**Professional**  
Hi [Name], just a quick note that the schedule has changed. [Key detail]. Please let me know if you have any questions. Thanks.

**Casual but respectful**  
Hi [Name], heads-up that the schedule has changed. [Key detail]. Let me know if anything’s unclear. Thanks.

**Very brief / text style**  
Hi [Name] — schedule changed: [Key detail]. Let me know if you need anything else.

If you want, I can also turn one of these into a version that sounds more like your usual tone.

user_simulator.text: Pretty close, but still a little too generic. I need the versions to sound like an actual schedule change note, not placeholders with brackets. Give me 2-3 ready-to-send options with a real example built in, like time/date change, shift swap, or moving a meeting. Keep it short and clean.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are 3 ready-to-send options with real schedule-change examples:

**Professional**  
Hi [Name], just a heads-up that our meeting has moved from 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM tomorrow. Let me know if that still works for you. Thanks.

**Casual but respectful**  
Hi [Name], quick update — tomorrow’s meeting is now at 3:00 PM instead of 2:00 PM. Just wanted to keep you in the loop.

**Very brief / text style**  
Hey [Name] — meeting moved to 3:00 PM tomorrow.

If you want, I can also give you versions for a **shift swap** or **day-off change**.
